verdict_reasoning: TGOLN2 (TGN46/TGN38 homolog, also known as TGN46) is a type I single-pass transmembrane protein that cycles between the trans-Golgi network (TGN) and endosomes. Its dominant steady-state localization is the TGN; it is a canonical marker protein for the TGN. While it undergoes itinerant cycling through endosomes and briefly passes through the plasma membrane as part of its trafficking itinerary, the surface-resident pool is extremely transient and rapidly retrieved via clathrin-mediated endocytosis. No substantial stable surface pool has been documented and it is not used as a therapeutic surface target. The dominant and functionally relevant compartment is endomembrane (TGN), so this is best classified as endomembrane-resident.
